rationale: The cited paper appears conceptually central rather than incidental: both papers study sequential consumer choices where moral considerations shape indulgent/hedonic versus restrained options, and the 2006 paper provides a self-concept-based moral licensing mechanism that plausibly informs the 2023 paper’s moral-appropriateness and moral-character account. Given this strong construct overlap in the notes (licensing, altruistic self-concept, luxury/indulgence vs. hedonic mood-repair choice), this pair should not be dropped as mention-only.
